name: Saule
description: Saule is the Baltic sun goddess, a central figure in Latvian and Lithuanian mythology. She embodies light, warmth, and life, and is seen as the protector of the weak and vulnerable. Saule is often depicted as a beautiful woman riding a chariot across the sky, drawn by horses, bringing daylight to the world. She is believed to possess immense power over the natural world, influencing the seasons, fertility, and the cycle of life and death. Her daily journey is a symbol of the sun's continuous cycle, representing hope and renewal. Saule is married to Mėnulis, the moon god, but their relationship is fraught with conflict, symbolizing the ever-changing phases of the moon and the sun's dominance. She is also the mother of Žemyna (Earth) and Vaiva (rainbow). Saule is revered for her justice and fairness, ensuring that good deeds are rewarded and evil is punished. She is invoked in prayers for health, prosperity, and protection. Her festivals are celebrated during the summer solstice, marking the peak of her power and the abundance of life. Saule's influence extends to various aspects of Baltic culture, including folklore, art, and music, where she remains a potent symbol of vitality and cosmic order.
subtitle: Sun Goddess of Light and Life

keyMyths.title: Saule and Mėnulis
keyMyths.description: Saule and Mėnulis were married, but Mėnulis' infidelity angered Saule, leading to their separation. This explains the eternal chase between the sun and the moon.

keyMyths.title: Saule's Journey
keyMyths.description: Each day, Saule rides her chariot across the sky, bringing light and warmth to the world. Her journey symbolizes the cycle of life and the power of the sun.

keyMyths.title: Saule and the Orphans
keyMyths.description: Saule is known to be a protector of orphans, ensuring they are cared for and treated with kindness. She rewards those who help the vulnerable.

sources.author: Marija Gimbutas
sources.work: The Goddesses and Gods of Old Europe
sources.description: Provides insights into the pre-Christian beliefs and the role of goddesses like Saule.
sources.text: Provides insights into the pre-Christian beliefs and the role of goddesses like Saule.

sources.author: Algirdas Julius Greimas
sources.description: Explores Lithuanian mythology and the significance of Saule.
